title: Ammannia portula
taxonomy: Ammannia portula is a flowering plant species within the family Lythraceae. It belongs to the order Myrtales. The plant's classification is further divided into the genus Ammannia and the species portula. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and phylogenetic relationships. Ammannia portula is native to tropical and subtropical regions.
uses: Ammannia portula is known for its various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional African medicine. It has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. Additionally, Ammannia portula is believed to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Further research is necessary to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential.

side_effects: Ammannia portula, also known as dwarf copper leaf, is a plant that may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals. Ingesting the plant can lead to gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. While it's primarily used as an ornamental plant, there is limited information on its potential side effects when used medicinally. intro: Ammannia auriculata, commonly known as Ammannia portula, is a flowering plant in the family Lythraceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and diarrhea, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Ammannia portula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as tonics and ointments.
